reasoning: On July 5, 2025, Elon Musk declared on X that he had formed the "America Party" in response to a feud with President Donald Trump over a sweeping domestic policy bill. However, despite the media cycle analyzing the viability of a third party, subsequent developments showed the initial announcement was little more than an impulsive tweet. Musk publicly denied early FEC filings associated with the party and no genuine filings, dedicated social media presence, or official website were ever created, remaining far short of the market's requirement for tangible evidence of party establishment. By August 20, 2025, reports from the Wall Street Journal indicated Musk had "quietly slowed down" the third-party effort and essentially abandoned it in favor of backing JD Vance for 2028. Since then, the idea has vanished from his public discourse. With just over two months remaining in 2025 and no infrastructure established, the likelihood of an official political party being formed is exceedingly low.
